subject: Statistics
title: The Obesity Paradox
description: A quick, practical guide to The Obesity Paradox.

hook.text: Overweight heart failure patients live longer than normal-weight ones. Does being overweight protect you?

buildup.text: Multiple studies found that moderate obesity was associated with better survival in heart failure and other diseases.

discovery.text: The paradox is likely selection bias: normal-weight patients with heart failure may have more aggressive disease.

twist.text: Collider bias, reverse causation, and weight measurement timing all contribute. The paradox likely isn't real.

climax.text: It's a masterclass in how observational data can produce paradoxical, misleading associations.

punchline.text: The data says overweight helps. The biology says otherwise. Trust the mechanism.

quiz.question: What likely explains the obesity paradox in heart failure patients?
options: Selection and collider bias — normal-weight patients with heart failure may have more aggressive disease
Being overweight genuinely protects the heart
The studies used incorrect weight measurements
